rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL ET AL VS. ANNE KIHAGI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notce Of Motion & Motion To Vacate Order Of November 26, 2024, For Failure To Adjudicate Jurisdictional Challenges; Violation Of Cal. Rules 3.1179 And 3.1184
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 15, 2025 line 1. DEFENDANT ANNE KIHAGI AKA ANNA KIHAGI AKA ANNA SWAIN AKA ANNE KIHAGI SWAIN AKA ANNA KIHAGI SWAIN, XELAN PROP 1, LLC, RENKA PROP, LLC, NOZARI 2, LLC, ZORIALL, LLC MOTION / NOTCE OF MOTION & MOTION TO VACATE ORDER OF NOVEMBER 26, 2024, FOR FAILURE TO ADJUDICATE JURISDICTIONAL CHALLENGES; VIOLATION OF CAL. RULES 3.1179 AND 3.1184 is CONTINUED to June 4, 2025 for the moving party to comply with SFLR 2.7B and provide courtesy copies of the moving papers no later than May 16, 2025 with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. (Oppositions received) =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CUSTOM CONSTRUCTION ENTERPRISES VS. JAMES LODUCA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement And Request For Attorneys Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 15, 2025 line 2. DEFENDANT JAMES LODUCA, CHARLES SMITH NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT AND REQUEST FOR ATTORNEYS FEES AND COSTS is GRANTED. The Court finds that the attorneys' fees provision as it related to the cross-complaint is not a material settlement term. (Lindsay v. Lewandowski (2006) 139 Cal.App.4th 1618, 1622; see also, Westlands Water District v. All Persons Interested (2023) 95 Cal.App.5th 98, 127 [defining "material term"].) The Court further finds that Plaintiff has not met the elements to obtain rescission. (Donovan v. RRL Corp. (2001) 26 Cal.4th 261, 263-64.) The Court does not find the resulting imbalance in the agreed exchange so severe that it would be unfair to require the defendant to perform, nor does it find that it is more advantageous to the other party. (Id. at 282.) The parties understood that it was Defendant's intent to proceed on their cross-claim. Defendant could win or they could lose. They could win on some of their claims or on just one of their claims, in which case the court could decide that there is no prevailing party. It has also not been established, through admissible evidence, that the insurance carrier will not pay the attorney's fees. Further, the Court finds that Plaintiff was aware, at the time the contract was made, that he only had limited knowledge with respect to the facts to which the mistake relates but treated his limited knowledge as sufficient. (Id. at 283; see Keane declaration, para's. 4-5.) Finally, the results here are not overly harsh or one sided as to be unconscionable. Plaintiff received $50,000 towards its $85,000 claim and the parties reserved their respective rights to pursue and defend the separate cross-complaint. Moreover, both parties retain the right to seek fees on the cross-complaint if it is the prevailing party. Defendant is awarded reasonable attorneys' fees in the amount of $4,750 and $80 in costs incurred to enforce the settlement agreement. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JENNIFER SARKANY ET AL VS. CHRISTIE BARRETT WEST ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 15, 2025 line 4. PLAINTIFF JENNIFER SARKANY, RAMSEY ABOUREMELEH, SANDRA FIERRO, NINA ROBIN MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is DENIED. There are triable issues of fact regarding whether the October 11, 2018 deed was accepted. To the extent that Defendants' declarations contradict prior testimony, these raise credibility issues that are better suited for the trier of fact. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: TENDERLOIN NEIGHBORHOOD DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION VS. ROBERT WILKINS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Plaintiff To: 1) Compel Responses By Defendant To Form Interrogatories - Unlawful Detainer (Set One) Without Objection; By Defendant To Form Interrogatories - General (Set One) Compel Responses By Defendant To Special Interrogatories (Set One) Without Objection; Compel Responses By Defendant To Request For Production Of Documents (Set One) Without Objection; And 2) Deem Admitted By Defendant Requests For Admission (Set One), And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 15, 2025 line 7. PLAINTIFF TENDERLOIN NEIGHBORHOOD DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION OF PLAINTIFF TO: 1) COMPEL RESPONSES BY DEFENDANT TO FORM INTERROGATORIES - UNLAWFUL DETAINER (SET ONE) WITHOUT OBJECTION; BY DEFENDANT TO FORM INTERROGATORIES - GENERAL (SET ONE) COMPEL RESPONSES BY DEFENDANT TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES (SET ONE) WITHOUT OBJECTION; COMPEL RESPONSES BY DEFENDANT TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS (SET ONE) WITHOUT OBJECTION; AND 2) DEEM ADMITTED BY DEFENDANT REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION (SET ONE), AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS is GRANTED in part, subject to opposition. Defendant shall serve complete verified responses without objection to Plaintiff's Form Interrogatories-Unlawful Detainer (Set One); Form Interrogatories-General (Set One); Special Interrogatories (Set One); and Request for Production of Documents (Set One) within 5 days of notice of entry of order. Truth of all matters in the Request for Admissions (Set One) are deemed admitted. Sanctions granted in the amount of $1060. Request to compel production of documents is denied as plaintiff has failed to cite any authority to justify this relief. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: 905 COLUMBUS AVENUE, LLC VS. JOHN COMPTON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 15, 2025 line 8. PLAINTIFF 905 COLUMBUS AVENUE, LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is DENIED. Plaintiff has not shifted its burden to show that the notice was served within one year after the rent became due, which is required to establish a prima facie case for unlawful detainer under CCP 1161(2). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
